text.title: AI Content Quality Review Output Contract
text.summary: Defines the shape of the rubric score, the inline annotation syntax, and the publish/revise/scrap decision rule.

section.title: Required rubric fields

list.item: piece_path (string, repo-relative)
icp (string, exact ICP declared in brief)
target_query (string, exact target keyword or job-to-be-done query)
section_scores (object with 7 keys, each 0-5): entity_density, citation_depth, original_pov, intro_tax, stat_verification, icp_fit, snippet_readiness
red_flags (array of objects: line_range, tag, severity)
verified_stats (array of objects: stat_text, source_url, verification_status)
original_pov_paragraph (string, the paragraph identified as `[[original-pov]]` or null)
decision (enum: publish, revise, scrap)

section.reference: Authoritative JSON Schema for the rubric output.

list.item: scrap when ANY: original_pov_paragraph is null AND piece >= 1500 words; entity_density score <= 1; >= 3 stat-unverified red flags affecting headline claim.
revise when ANY: any section_score <= 2; >= 1 forbidden intro opener; original_pov_paragraph is null on a piece 600-1499 words; <5 primary citations on a piece >= 1500 words.
publish when ALL: every section_score >= 3; original_pov_paragraph non-null; zero unverified stats in headline claims; citation count meets r2 floor.

section.title: Inline annotation syntax

list.item: [[entity-thin]]: section below the 2-per-100-words floor (r1).
[[citation-needed]]: claim of fact without a primary-source link (r2).
[[original-pov]]: paragraph identified as the original-POV anchor (r3, required).
[[intro-tax]]: forbidden generic opener detected (r4).
[[stat-unverified]]: numeric stat without traceable source (r5).

section.reference: Annotation usage rules and edge cases.

antipattern.description: Reviewer emits a `publish` decision while original_pov_paragraph is null on a piece >= 600 words.
antipattern.reason: r3 violation. Original POV is a hard publish gate; no exceptions.

antipattern.description: Verified_stats array empty while body contains numeric statistics.
antipattern.reason: r5 evasion. Stats must be enumerated even if all flagged unverified.

antipattern.description: Rubric score given a 4 or 5 with no red_flags listed but section has >= 1 known antipattern (e.g. generic intro opener detected).
antipattern.reason: Score / red-flag mismatch defeats the rubric. Scores derive from red-flag counts.

list.item: Every section_score has supporting red_flags or an explicit "no flags" note.
Original-POV paragraph identified verbatim (not paraphrased).
Every stat in the body appears in verified_stats with status.
Decision matches the decision rule table exactly.
ICP and target_query echoed back from the brief — not paraphrased by the reviewer.
